commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Slack draft preview dispatch to track narrative versus tool-progress draft state, delete `Workingâ€¦` bullet previews on transitions and final cleanup, update Slack docs/tests/changelog, and remove stale raw-fetch guard allowlist entries.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main writes tool-progress bullets into the Slack draft stream and rotates the message at boundaries without deleting it. The test blocker is also source-reproducible because the PR adds a new mocked-module import without updating the existing dispatch test mock.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the PR branch test mock and add focused Slack dispatch coverage for the new delete path.

target.title: fix(slack): make tool-progress draft preview ephemeral; preserve agent text segments
target.author: shadowwalker2014

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: ShadowWalker2014
commands.command: iteration 3 addressed in commit `16891a2217` (now `44256f04f9` after cleanup): **[p2] narrativeâ†’bullets rotation drops or corrupts pending narrative text** you're right â€” `pushpreviewtoolprogress()` was calling `forcenewmessage()` without first awaiting any pending or in-flight narrative `chat.update`. because `draftstream.update()` is throttled and async, an in-flight narrative send could land *after* `forcenewmessage()` reset `lastsenttext`/`pendingtext`/`messageid`, leaving the agent's text segment either silently discarded (pending text dropped on `loop.resetpending()`) or written into what becomes the bullet preview slot â€” and then deleted at end-of-turn when `deletebulletpreview()` tears it down. fix: `pushpreviewtoolprogress()` is now `async`. before `forcenewmessage()` it awaits `draftstream.flush()`, which drains `pendingtext` and `inflightpromise` without stopping the loop (`clear()` is still off-limits â€” it calls `discardpending â†’ stop`). all six call sites are already in async callbacks (`ontoolstart`, `onitemevent`, `onplanupdate`, `onapprovalevent`, `oncommandoutput`, `onpatchsummary`), so they now `await pushpreviewtoolprogress(...)`. symmetric fix in `ondraftboundary`'s narrative branch: same race exists when an assistant-message boundary rotates a narrative draft. now also awaits `flush()` before `forcenewmessage()` so the pending agent text segment lands as a permanent message before id rotation. **rebase + housekeeping** - rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`00de585574`). resolved conflicts in `dispatch.ts`, `dispatch.streaming.test.ts`, and `docs/channels/slack.md` â€” all upstream simplifications kept; my behavior changes preserved on top. - dropped stale `chutes/models.ts` and `huggingface/models.ts` allowlist entries from `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` â€” upstream removed those raw fetches between rebase points. **test status** `pnpm test extensions/slack`: 89 files / 898 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean across all touched files. pushed (force-with-lease) to `44256f04f9`. ci re-running. the remaining ci failure on the previous run (`extension-runtime-dependencies.contract.test.ts > extensions/tlon does not keep unused direct runtime dependencies`) is pre-existing on `upstream/main` and unrelated to this pr.
commands.freeform_prompt: iteration 3 addressed in commit `16891a2217` (now `44256f04f9` after cleanup): **[P2] Narrativeâ†’bullets rotation drops or corrupts pending narrative text** You're right â€” `pushPreviewToolProgress()` was calling `forceNewMessage()` without first awaiting any pending or in-flight narrative `chat.update`. Because `draftStream.update()` is throttled and async, an in-flight narrative send could land *after* `forceNewMessage()` reset `lastSentText`/`pendingText`/`messageId`, leaving the agent's text segment either silently discarded (pending text dropped on `loop.resetPending()`) or written into what becomes the bullet preview slot â€” and then deleted at end-of-turn when `deleteBulletPreview()` tears it down. Fix: `pushPreviewToolProgress()` is now `async`. Before `forceNewMessage()` it awaits `draftStream.flush()`, which drains `pendingText` and `inFlightPromise` without stopping the loop (`clear()` is still off-limits â€” it calls `discardPending â†’ stop`). All six call sites are already in async callbacks (`onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary`), so they now `await pushPreviewToolProgress(...)`. Symmetric fix in `onDraftBoundary`'s narrative branch: same race exists when an assistant-message boundary rotates a narrative draft. Now also awaits `flush()` before `forceNewMessage()` so the pending agent text segment lands as a permanent message before id rotation. **Rebase + housekeeping** - Rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`00de585574`). Resolved conflicts in `dispatch.ts`, `dispatch.streaming.test.ts`, and `docs/channels/slack.md` â€” all upstream simplifications kept; my behavior changes preserved on top. - Dropped stale `chutes/models.ts` and `huggingface/models.ts` allowlist entries from `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` â€” upstream removed those raw fetches between rebase points. **Test status** `pnpm test extensions/slack`: 89 files / 898 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean across all touched files. Pushed (force-with-lease) to `44256f04f9`. CI re-running. The remaining CI failure on the previous run (`extension-runtime-dependencies.contract.test.ts > extensions/tlon does not keep unused direct runtime dependencies`) is pre-existing on `upstream/main` and unrelated to this PR.

commands.author: ShadowWalker2014
commands.command: iteration 2 addressed in commit `22e07c08fd`: **[p2] in-flight bullet sends â†’ orphan preview race** you're right â€” `deletebulletpreview()` was capturing ids and calling `forcenewmessage()` without draining the draft loop's in-flight send. `workingâ€¦` sends in flight when the helper ran could complete after we'd reset, leaving an orphan preview. fix: `deletebulletpreview()` now awaits `draftstream.flush()` first. `flush()` drains pending text and waits for in-flight sends to settle, so when `channelid`/`messageid` are read next they point to the real slack message. `forcenewmessage` + `chat.delete` then completes the deletion. `clear()` is still off-limits because it calls `discardpending` â†’ `stop`, but `flush` keeps the loop reusable. **[p3] changelog entry** added under `unreleased` / `fixes` (`channels/slack: â€¦`). single-line, no maintainer attribution. **drive-by** `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` allowlist had stale line numbers for `extensions/chutes/models.ts` and `extensions/huggingface/models.ts` (off-by-one) after upstream's `52cd76a9e2` (`fix(providers): isolate model discovery test env guards`) shifted those files by an added import. updated `(535, 542, 142)` â†’ `(536, 543, 143)` so `check-additional-boundaries` passes. happy to split this off into its own pr if preferred â€” flagging here so it's visible. **test status** 89 files / 897 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean. pushed; ci re-running.
commands.freeform_prompt: iteration 2 addressed in commit `22e07c08fd`: **[P2] In-flight bullet sends â†’ orphan preview race** You're right â€” `deleteBulletPreview()` was capturing IDs and calling `forceNewMessage()` without draining the draft loop's in-flight send. `Workingâ€¦` sends in flight when the helper ran could complete after we'd reset, leaving an orphan preview. Fix: `deleteBulletPreview()` now awaits `draftStream.flush()` FIRST. `flush()` drains pending text and waits for in-flight sends to settle, so when `channelId`/`messageId` are read next they point to the real Slack message. `forceNewMessage` + `chat.delete` then completes the deletion. `clear()` is still off-limits because it calls `discardPending` â†’ `stop`, but `flush` keeps the loop reusable. **[P3] CHANGELOG entry** Added under `Unreleased` / `Fixes` (`Channels/Slack: â€¦`). Single-line, no maintainer attribution. **Drive-by** `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` allowlist had stale line numbers for `extensions/chutes/models.ts` and `extensions/huggingface/models.ts` (off-by-one) after upstream's `52cd76a9e2` (`fix(providers): isolate model discovery test env guards`) shifted those files by an added import. Updated `(535, 542, 142)` â†’ `(536, 543, 143)` so `check-additional-boundaries` passes. Happy to split this off into its own PR if preferred â€” flagging here so it's visible. **Test status** 89 files / 897 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean. Pushed; CI re-running.

commands.author: ShadowWalker2014
commands.command: thanks for the review â€” both findings addressed in the latest commit: **[p2] `clear()` stopping the draft stream mid-turn** you're right, `draftstream.clear()` calls `discardpending()` which calls `stop()`. replaced `clear()` at all four mid-turn transition sites with a new local helper `deletebulletpreview()` that: 1. captures `channelid` / `messageid` from the draftstream 2. calls `draftstream.forcenewmessage()` to reset cached ids (does **not** stop the loop) 3. awaits `chat.delete` on the captured ids the stream stays usable after deletion, so a later `update()` (narrative text, another tool burst) correctly posts a fresh permanent message. sites updated: - `updatedraftfrompartial` bulletsâ†’narrative transition - `ondraftboundary` bullets case - `kind === "final"` + bullets transition - `finally` cleanup **[p3] dm streaming docs** updated `docs/channels/slack.md:652` so the dm section matches the new behaviour: top-level dms do show the live preview now, edited via `chat.update` while tools run and deleted at end of turn. removed the outdated 'use thread replies or `typingreaction`' guidance. **test status:** 89 files / 894 tests pass; `oxfmt --check` clean. commit pushed; ci re-running.
commands.freeform_prompt: thanks for the review â€” both findings addressed in the latest commit: **[P2] `clear()` stopping the draft stream mid-turn** You're right, `draftStream.clear()` calls `discardPending()` which calls `stop()`. Replaced `clear()` at all four mid-turn transition sites with a new local helper `deleteBulletPreview()` that: 1. Captures `channelId` / `messageId` from the draftStream 2. Calls `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` to reset cached IDs (does **not** stop the loop) 3. Awaits `chat.delete` on the captured IDs The stream stays usable after deletion, so a later `update()` (narrative text, another tool burst) correctly posts a fresh permanent message. Sites updated: - `updateDraftFromPartial` bulletsâ†’narrative transition - `onDraftBoundary` bullets case - `kind === "final"` + bullets transition - `finally` cleanup **[P3] DM streaming docs** Updated `docs/channels/slack.md:652` so the DM section matches the new behaviour: top-level DMs do show the live preview now, edited via `chat.update` while tools run and deleted at end of turn. Removed the outdated 'use thread replies or `typingReaction`' guidance. **Test status:** 89 files / 894 tests pass; `oxfmt --check` clean. Commit pushed; CI re-running.
